# test-3d-builder — Copilot Instructions

## Scope
These instructions apply to the entire repository.

## Project Stack
- Next.js App Router (v16), React 19, TypeScript
- React Three Fiber + Three.js + drei for 3D
- Zustand for configurator state
- Tailwind CSS v4 for UI

## Code Style Expectations
- Prefer clear, small, single-purpose functions.
- Avoid redundant comments.
- Do not add comments for obvious code generated by models.
- Add comments only when logic is non-trivial (geometry math, transform rules, constraints).
- Preserve existing naming and folder conventions (feature-based modules in src/features).
- Keep changes incremental; avoid broad rewrites unless explicitly requested.

## 3D/Configurator Rules
- Keep geometry and visual behavior deterministic.
- Do not hardcode random visual values without explanation.
- Gate rendering must stay type-driven (`tilt`, `double-wing`, `sectional`).
- If adding a new gate/material/roof option, update all related places:
  - `src/store/types.ts`
  - `src/config/settings.ts`
  - relevant UI panels and 3D model components
- Keep camera constraints safe (no clipping through model, no underground camera).

## Mobile/Responsive Rules
- Mobile-first layout for configurator pages.
- Avoid fixed `100vh/100vw` on containers when it breaks mobile browsers.
- Use `dvh` when viewport height behavior matters.
- Ensure side panels and scene remain usable on screens < 768px.

## Quality Gates
Before finishing a task:
1. Check diagnostics for edited files.
2. Ensure no new TypeScript or lint errors introduced by the change.
3. Keep accessibility basics in forms/inputs (`aria-label` where needed).

## Pipeline Snapshot
- Dev: `npm run dev`
- Build: `npm run build`
- Start (prod): `npm run start`
- Lint: `npm run lint`

## Functional Snapshot
- Interactive 3D garage scene with controlled orbit camera.
- Configurable dimensions, roof, gates, construction/material options.
- Sprite-based materials and configurable ground sprite.
- Multiple gate types with dedicated visual details per type.
- Zustand-driven config state shared between UI panels and 3D scene.
